title: Bridge Essay: Role of Forkhead Box Protein O1 (FoxO1) in Stroke: A Literature Review.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Liberation/Enlightenment** (Phase 15) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Liberation/Enlightenment to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Role of Forkhead Box Protein O1 (FoxO1) in Stroke: A Literature Review.** — Aging Dis. PMID: 35371601. DOI: 10.14336/AD.2021.0826. Stroke is one of the most prevalent causes of death around the world. When astroke occurs, many cellular signaling cascades and regulators are activated, which results in severe cellular dysfunction and debilitating long-term disability. One crucial regulator of cell fate and function is mammalian Forkhead box protein O1 (FoxO1). Many studies have found FoxO1 to be implicated in many cellular processes, including regulating gluconeogenesis and glycogenolysis. During astroke, modifications of F
